summary.compositionGateDetails: Logo image blows up to fill entire nav bar height at both desktop and mobile breakpoints. The overall shape of the header changes from a compact horizontal bar (Figma) to a tall block dominated by an oversized logo (implementation). This is a Content-Resilience failure: the code lacks proper height constraints on the logo, so any image that is not naturally wide and short will break the proportions.

issues.figmaValue: Desktop logo: 57.685px tall (66.84px bee + 148.6px wordmark, total ~215px wide). Mobile logo: 25.599px tall (99.844px wide). Logo is a compact element within a horizontally-oriented nav bar.
issues.implementationValue: Logo uses self-stretch + **:h-full with NO max-height. Any image fills the entire flex container height, causing the nav bar to expand to accommodate the image's natural height. With a square favicon, the logo becomes ~300-400px tall, completely breaking the header proportions.
issues.difference: Structural: nav bar goes from ~90px tall compact bar (Figma) to ~400px+ tall block (implementation)
issues.recommendation: Add max-height constraints to the logo link and/or image. The logo needs to be constrained so it never exceeds its Figma dimensions regardless of the uploaded image's aspect ratio. Suggested fix in HeaderMain.php line 60: change `class="block self-stretch w-auto **:w-auto **:max-w-full **:h-full shrink-0"` to include a max-height, e.g. `class="block self-stretch w-auto **:w-auto **:max-w-full **:h-full **:max-h-[58px] max-md:**:max-h-[26px] shrink-0"`. Also add `**:object-contain` to ensure the image scales proportionally within the constraint.
issues.contentResilienceRule: This was previously dismissed as a 'test content issue' (DQA-001 in prior report). Per the Content-Resilience Rule: the design must look appropriate regardless of what content is used. The code lacks proper CSS constraints (max-height) so the design breaks with any image that is not naturally the exact right aspect ratio. The fix is to add CSS constraints, not to require a specific image.
issues.verification: After fix, the logo should be constrained to ~58px tall on desktop and ~26px on mobile, matching Figma proportions. The nav bar should appear as a compact horizontal bar regardless of the uploaded logo image's aspect ratio.

passedChecks.description: Container wrapper present. Header.php wraps children in <div class='container'>. Header is contained, not full-width.

Desktop two-row layout structure is correct. Top utility row: search (250px, border-b) + vertical separator + SEP link with arrow. Bottom row: nav items with chevrons, right-aligned. Logo on left. Structure matches Figma aside from logo size.
Nav bar background: bg-charcoal (#1f1f1d) vs Figma #1e1e1c. 1-unit RGB difference, imperceptible and within tolerance.
Nav bar rounded corners: rounded-[25px]. Matches Figma exactly.
Nav bar padding: px-8 (32px) py-4 (16px). Figma: px-32 py-16. Exact match.
Nav items: font-sans font-medium text-base (16px) leading-[1.16] uppercase text-white. Matches Figma General_Sans:Medium 16px.
Search form with input field and submit button with SearchIcon.svg. Width 250px, border-b border-white, pb-1. Matches Figma.
SEP link renders with ExternalArrow.svg. gap-2 (8px) between text and icon. Matches Figma gap-8.
Gap between utility row and nav row: gap-6 (24px). Figma: gap-24. Exact match.
Gap between nav items: gap-8 (32px). Figma: gap-32. Exact match.
Vertical separator between search and SEP: w-px h-5 bg-white. Matches Figma.
SEP mobile button above nav bar: bg-charcoal, rounded-full, p-4 (16px), gap-3, text-white uppercase + ExternalArrow.svg. Figma: rounded-119px, p-16. Matches.
Gap between SEP button and nav bar: gap-2 (8px). Figma: gap-8. Exact match.
Mobile toggle button renders with hamburger icon. md:hidden, aria-controls, aria-expanded attributes present.

contentResilienceAnalysis.rule: The design must look appropriate regardless of what content is used. Do NOT dismiss visual problems as test content issues.
contentResilienceAnalysis.finding: The logo link uses self-stretch + **:h-full with no max-height constraint. This means any image that is not naturally wide and short (like the Figma reference logo) will blow up to fill the entire nav bar, breaking the header's compact proportions. A square favicon, a tall portrait image, or any non-horizontal image will cause the same problem.
contentResilienceAnalysis.previousReport: The prior QA report (design-qa-2026-02-09-203303.json) dismissed this as DQA-001 severity:minor with note 'Test content issue - wrong image uploaded, not a code issue'. This violates the Content-Resilience Rule.
contentResilienceAnalysis.correctClassification: This is a CODE ISSUE (severity: critical). The fix is to add max-height constraints so the logo is properly constrained regardless of the uploaded image's aspect ratio.

codeVerification.rootCause: HeaderMain.php line 60: Logo link class 'block self-stretch w-auto **:w-auto **:max-w-full **:h-full shrink-0' uses self-stretch + **:h-full without any max-height constraint. The flex container (items-center justify-between on line 53) stretches to accommodate the tallest item, creating a feedback loop where the logo image grows to fill available height, which increases the container height, which makes the logo grow more.
